CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-03-2017 Continued To Dec-20-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff'S Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kelly Collins, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3914, for defendant and moving party;
Patricia Hewlett, Tel. 415.515.7860, plaintiff appearing on her own behalf.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: "Plaintiff Patricia Hewlett's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is denied. The proposed second amended complaint suffers from the same deficiencies as the first amended complaint and fails to state any cognizable claims. All harm complained of in the proposed second amended complaint appears to be harm allegedly committed against Henry Solorzano. Ms. Hewlett lacks standing or authority to sue on behalf of Mr. Solorzano. Moreover, to the extent that the proposed second amended complaint alleges that any actions taken in connection with Mr. Solorzano's conservatorship case were improper, the proposed second amended complaint is an impermissible collateral attack on the orders in the conservatorship case." The moving party is to submit a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kelly Collins, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3914, for defendant and moving party;
Patricia Hewlett, Tel. 415.515.7860, plaintiff appearing on her own behalf.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as follows: "Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to all three causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by Patricia Hewlett on her own behalf and on behalf of Henry Solorzano is sustained without leave to amend as to all three causes of action. All harm complained of in the first amended complaint appears to be harm allegedly committed against Mr. Solorzano. Ms. Hewlett lacks standing or authority to sue on behalf of Mr. Solorzano. Moreover, to the extent that the first amended complaint alleges that any actions taken in connection with Mr. Solorzano's conservatorship case were improper, the first amended complaint is an impermissible collateral attack on the orders in the conservatorship case. Ms. Hewlett's request to file a second amended complaint is denied because her proposed second amended complaint suffers from the same deficiencies as the first amended complaint." The moving party is to submit a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org.
